criteria_verification.evidence: Created src/forge/pipeline/stage_ordering_guard.py with `class StageOrderingGuard` exported via `__all__`. The companion `StageLogReader` Protocol is defined in the same module so callers have a single import surface.
criteria_verification.notes: Created src/forge/pipeline/stage_ordering_guard.py with `class StageOrderingGuard` exported via `__all__`. The companion `StageLogReader` Protocol is defined in the same module so callers have a single import surface.

criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented `StageOrderingGuard.next_dispatchable(build_id, stage_log_reader) -> set[StageClass]`. Walks every `StageClass` and delegates to `is_dispatchable`; for per-feature non-PR stages it includes the stage iff at least one feature in the catalogue has prerequisites met. Verified by `TestNextDispatchable` (4 tests covering empty log, post-product-owner, per-feature any-feature semantics, and PR review all-features semantics).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented `StageOrderingGuard.next_dispatchable(build_id, stage_log_reader) -> set[StageClass]`. Walks every `StageClass` and delegates to `is_dispatchable`; for per-feature non-PR stages it includes the stage iff at least one feature in the catalogue has prerequisites met. Verified by `TestNextDispatchable` (4 tests covering empty log, post-product-owner, per-feature any-feature semantics, and PR review all-features semantics).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Method `is_dispatchable(build_id, stage: StageClass, feature_id: str | None = None) -> bool`
criteria_verification.evidence: Implemented `is_dispatchable(build_id, stage, stage_log_reader, feature_id=None) -> bool`. Note: the AC signature was extended with the `stage_log_reader` parameter to keep the guard pure (no constructor state) — this is consistent with the task's Implementation Notes ("Inject a StageLogReader Protocol") and the explicit reader parameter on `next_dispatchable`. The decision tree mirrors the seven Group B rows; per-feature prerequisites are scoped at the same `feature_id`. Verified by 14 parametrized tests in `TestGroupBPrerequisiteRows` (negative + positive for every row).
criteria_verification.notes: Implemented `is_dispatchable(build_id, stage, stage_log_reader, feature_id=None) -> bool`. Note: the AC signature was extended with the `stage_log_reader` parameter to keep the guard pure (no constructor state) — this is consistent with the task's Implementation Notes ("Inject a StageLogReader Protocol") and the explicit reader parameter on `next_dispatchable`. The decision tree mirrors the seven Group B rows; per-feature prerequisites are scoped at the same `feature_id`. Verified by 14 parametrized tests in `TestGroupBPrerequisiteRows` (negative + positive for every row).

criteria_verification.evidence: `is_dispatchable` accepts `feature_id` and refuses per-feature stages without one (safe default to prevent cross-feature prerequisite leaks). Per-feature prerequisites are checked at the same scoped `feature_id` (rows 5 and 6 "for that feature"). Covered by `TestPerFeatureScoping` (3 tests: feature-plan-per-feature, autobuild-per-feature, refusal when feature_id missing).
criteria_verification.notes: `is_dispatchable` accepts `feature_id` and refuses per-feature stages without one (safe default to prevent cross-feature prerequisite leaks). Per-feature prerequisites are checked at the same scoped `feature_id` (rows 5 and 6 "for that feature"). Covered by `TestPerFeatureScoping` (3 tests: feature-plan-per-feature, autobuild-per-feature, refusal when feature_id missing).

criteria_verification.evidence: PR review is special-cased: it pulls `feature_catalogue(build_id)` from the reader and uses `all(...)` over `is_approved(build_id, AUTOBUILD, feature_id=fid)` for every feature. Empty catalogue is non-dispatchable (defends TASK-MAG7-005 boundary). Verified by `TestPullRequestReviewMultiFeature` (3 tests: blocked-with-one-missing, dispatchable-when-all-approved, blocked-on-empty-catalogue).
criteria_verification.notes: PR review is special-cased: it pulls `feature_catalogue(build_id)` from the reader and uses `all(...)` over `is_approved(build_id, AUTOBUILD, feature_id=fid)` for every feature. Empty catalogue is non-dispatchable (defends TASK-MAG7-005 boundary). Verified by `TestPullRequestReviewMultiFeature` (3 tests: blocked-with-one-missing, dispatchable-when-all-approved, blocked-on-empty-catalogue).

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests cover all seven prerequisite rows from the Scenario Outline
criteria_verification.evidence: `TestGroupBPrerequisiteRows` parametrize table lists all seven rows verbatim (architect←product-owner through pull-request←autobuild) with both negative and positive assertions per row — 14 test cases total. The class docstring reproduces the table for review parity.
criteria_verification.notes: `TestGroupBPrerequisiteRows` parametrize table lists all seven rows verbatim (architect←product-owner through pull-request←autobuild) with both negative and positive assertions per row — 14 test cases total. The class docstring reproduces the table for review parity.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unit tests cover the multi-feature case (PR review requires all autobuilds)
criteria_verification.evidence: `TestPullRequestReviewMultiFeature.test_pr_review_blocked_when_one_of_several_autobuilds_missing` and `test_pr_review_dispatchable_when_every_autobuild_approved` exercise a 2-feature catalogue, asserting the false→true transition as the second autobuild lands. `TestNextDispatchable.test_pr_review_in_set_only_when_every_feature_autobuilt` covers the same property through the aggregator method.
criteria_verification.notes: `TestPullRequestReviewMultiFeature.test_pr_review_blocked_when_one_of_several_autobuilds_missing` and `test_pr_review_dispatchable_when_every_autobuild_approved` exercise a 2-feature catalogue, asserting the false→true transition as the second autobuild lands. `TestNextDispatchable.test_pr_review_in_set_only_when_every_feature_autobuilt` covers the same property through the aggregator method.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Pure function — no I/O except via the injected `stage_log_reader` Protocol
criteria_verification.evidence: `StageOrderingGuard` has no constructor state (verified by `test_guard_constructs_without_arguments`); both methods are sync and depend only on the injected `StageLogReader`. No async, no SQLite import, no filesystem access. `TestPureFunctionProperty.test_guard_only_calls_protocol_methods` uses a `RecordingReader` whose `__getattr__` raises to prove the guard touches only `is_approved` and `feature_catalogue`.
criteria_verification.notes: `StageOrderingGuard` has no constructor state (verified by `test_guard_constructs_without_arguments`); both methods are sync and depend only on the injected `StageLogReader`. issues.description:
Advisory (non-blocking): task-work produced a report with 2 of 3 expected agent invocations. Missing phases: 3 (Implementation). Consider invoking these agents via the Task tool to strengthen stack-specific quality:
- Phase 3: `python-api-specialist` (Implementation)
No seam tests detected for cross-boundary feature. Consider adding seam/contract/boundary tests to validate technology boundary interactions. This is a recommendation, not a blocking gate.

rationale: All quality gates passed. Independent verification confirmed. All acceptance criteria met. Architecture context: 267 chars provided.
